untranslatable_reason: The signal requires dynamic Bollinger Band construction with a specific lookback period and half-life (both marked AUTHOR_SILENT), as well as precise entry at ±2 standard deviations and exit at ±1 standard deviation from a moving average. The allowed indicator set provides static bb_upper/bb_lower/bb_middle (likely 20-period default) but does not expose the underlying moving average period, standard deviation multipliers, or ability to define custom bands with author-specified parameters. Cannot accurately translate without knowing the exact lookback_period, half_life, and whether these bands use SMA or EMA.
translation_notes: Approximation using standard Bollinger Bands (bb_upper/bb_lower/bb_middle) provided in allowed indicators. True signal requires configurable Bollinger Band parameters (lookback, standard deviation multipliers) that are not exposed. The exit condition targets ±1 std dev but allowed indicators only provide ±2 std dev bands. Sharpe ratio degradation (-3.0 with 1bp costs) suggests this is a high-frequency, transaction-cost-sensitive strategy that may not be reliably backtestable without precise parameter matching.
